name: OpenClaw
description: The orchestration runtime that powers vamabuilds itself. Runs persistent AI agents, manages sessions, and connects tools across an agentic org.
reviewNote: We run this in production. It handles session management, tool routing, and agent orchestration in the vamabuilds stack.

name: Anthropic Claude API
description: The model API behind most of the vamabuilds agentic stack. Strong reasoning, large context window, and consistent instruction-following for complex agent tasks.
reviewNote: Primary LLM for all vamabuilds agents. Tested extensively in multi-step orchestration and document-heavy tasks.

name: xurl
description: CLI tool for authenticated X (Twitter) API v2 requests. Posts, replies, searches, and DMs from the terminal or a script.
reviewNote: Used in the vamabuilds social stack. Clean interface, handles auth well, no hidden permissions beyond what the X API requires.

name: CrewAI
description: Multi-agent framework focused on role-based collaboration. Defines agents with personas, goals, and tools, then coordinates them on tasks.
reviewNote: Tested for parallel research and writing tasks. Role definition works well. Watch the token consumption on large crews.

name: GitHub
description: Version control and CI/CD backbone of the vamabuilds stack. All agent-produced code is committed, reviewed, and deployed through GitHub.
reviewNote: Core infrastructure. All vamabuilds repos live here. GitHub Actions handles CI; branches and commits are the canonical audit trail for agent builds.

name: Vercel
description: Deployment platform for all vamabuilds web properties. Handles build, preview, and production deployments from GitHub pushes with zero-config Next.js support.
reviewNote: All vamabuilds sites deploy here in production. Instant rollbacks, preview environments per branch, and clean CLI integration for agent-driven deploys.

name: Paperclip
description: Agentic company OS. Provides a structured runtime for multi-agent organizations with built-in company context, task routing, and agent coordination primitives.
reviewNote: In our evaluation. Built by @dotta — directly aligned with the agentic org architecture vamabuilds runs.

name: DevClaw
description: OpenClaw plugin that turns the orchestrator agent into a development manager. Handles multi-project dev and QA pipeline orchestration.
reviewNote: OpenClaw ecosystem plugin. Relevant for agentic orgs running multi-project dev pipelines.

name: Grok
description: xAI's frontier model. Strong reasoning and real-time web access. Used in the vamabuilds stack for tasks requiring up-to-date information and fast inference.
reviewNote: xAI model. Part of the vamabuilds LLM stack alongside Claude.

name: PostHog
description: Open-source product analytics. Tracks events, funnels, session recordings, and feature flags in one platform. Self-hostable or cloud.
reviewNote: Used for product analytics across the vamabuilds stack.

name: Supabase
description: Open-source Firebase alternative. Postgres database, auth, storage, and edge functions in one platform. Used for backend data layer in agentic workflows.
reviewNote: Used for database and auth in the vamabuilds stack.

name: Tailscale
description: Zero-config VPN built on WireGuard. Connects machines, agents, and services into a private network without exposing ports. Essential for secure agentic infra.
reviewNote: Used to secure the vamabuilds infrastructure network.

name: Vultr
description: Cloud infrastructure provider. Hosts the VPS running the vamabuilds agentic stack. Simple pricing, global data centers, and straightforward VM provisioning.
reviewNote: Our primary VPS host. The vamabuilds agent runtime runs on a Vultr instance.

name: Anthropic Knowledge Work Plugins
description: Open-source Claude plugins for knowledge workers. 11 plugins covering productivity, sales, and more — bundles skills, connectors, slash commands, and sub-agents for specific job functions. Compatible with Claude Cowork and Claude Code.
reviewNote: Official Anthropic plugins. Extend Claude with role-specific workflows, connectors (Slack, Notion, Linear, etc.), and slash commands.
